Title: Diverse soil protists show auxin regulated growth in partnership with auxin-producing bacteria

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate auxin-response/transport-associated homolog whose transcript abundance increased with IAA treatment, supporting protist auxin responsiveness.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIG homolog was identified among auxin-related candidate genes in protist assemblies. 2. In Colpoda sp., BIG was among auxin-pathway homologs differentially expressed under IAA treatment. 3. Its presence supports conservation of auxin-related cellular machinery in protists.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome homology search against Arabidopsis auxin genes; RNA-seq differential expression under IAA treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: elevated under IAA treatment in Colpoda sp.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes protist growth and delays encystment.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role inferred by homology only; no direct mechanistic assay in protists.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate auxin transport-related homolog transcriptionally responsive to IAA in Colpoda sp.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAR3 homolog was identified among conserved auxin-related genes in protists. 2. It was among IAA-pathway homologs with altered transcript abundance in Colpoda sp. under IAA treatment. 3. Supports the existence of auxin-responsive cellular systems in heterotrophic protists.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome homology search; RNA-seq differential expression

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Protists including Colpoda sp., Ochromonas sp. 1, Ochromonas sp. 2, Thaumatomastix sp.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate auxin metabolism/homeostasis gene conserved across protist assemblies and IAA-responsive in Colpoda sp.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ILR1 homologs were among 13 auxin-related genes present in all four protist assemblies. 2. ILR1 was among the Colpoda auxin-pathway homologs differentially expressed in response to IAA. 3. The finding supports conserved auxin processing/homeostasis functions in protists.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tBLASTn homology search against Arabidopsis genes; RNA-seq differential expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: elevated with IAA in Colpoda sp. (among 11 of 12 auxin-homolog transcripts increased)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function assigned by homology to plant auxin conjugate-processing genes.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate auxin homeostasis gene responsive to exogenous IAA in Colpoda sp.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAR3/ILL5 homolog was detected among Colpoda auxin-related genes. 2. Its transcript abundance changed under IAA treatment. 3. Supports involvement of auxin processing/homeostasis genes in protist IAA response.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome homology search; RNA-seq differential expression

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate auxin homeostasis-related homolog with IAA-responsive expression in Colpoda sp.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ILL family homologs were identified in Colpoda sp. 2. These genes were among auxin-pathway candidates differentially expressed after IAA treatment. 3. They support conservation of auxin metabolism modules in heterotrophic protists.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genome homology search; RNA-seq differential expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact family member resolution in protists is uncertain.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Protists including Colpoda sp., Ochromonas sp. 1, Ochromonas sp. 2, Thaumatomastix sp.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conserved candidate auxin redox homeostasis gene in protists, supporting auxin-related metabolic capacity.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAR4 homologs were among 13 auxin-related genes conserved across all four protist assemblies. 2. The paper interprets them as genes involved in auxin redox homeostasis. 3. Their conservation supports shared auxin-response modules across distant protist lineages.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct biochemical validation in protists.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Protists including Colpoda sp., Ochromonas sp. 1, Ochromonas sp. 2, Thaumatomastix sp.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conserved candidate auxin metabolism gene supporting protist IAA processing capacity.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAMT homologs were among the 13 auxin-related genes found in all four protist genomes analyzed. 2. The paper assigns these homologs to auxin processing and metabolism. 3. Their presence supports protist capacity for auxin-related metabolic handling.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Protists including Colpoda sp., Ochromonas sp. 1, Ochromonas sp. 2, Thaumatomastix sp.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate downstream auxin gene regulation component conserved in protists.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPR2/4 homologs were among 13 auxin-related genes shared across all four protist assemblies. 2. The paper classifies them as involved in downstream gene regulation. 3. Their presence supports a conserved gene-regulatory response framework associated with auxin in protists.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct regulatory assay in protists.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Protists including Colpoda sp., Ochromonas sp. 1, Ochromonas sp. 2, Thaumatomastix sp.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate downstream auxin regulatory component conserved across protists.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAC3B homologs were found in all four protist genome assemblies examined. 2. The paper interprets them as involved in downstream gene regulation. 3. This supports the existence of auxin-responsive regulatory machinery outside plants.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role in protists remains inferential.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IAA-responsive cell-cycle gene associated with protist growth response.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYC2 was among differentially abundant transcripts under IAA treatment in Colpoda sp. 2. The paper links it to cell cycle progression. 3. This supports a mechanism whereby IAA promotes protist growth via cell-cycle regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression under IAA treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because authors explicitly relate it to IAA-induced growth response.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IAA-responsive cell-cycle gene associated with enhanced protist growth.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDC20 transcript abundance changed significantly in response to IAA in Colpoda sp. 2. The paper links CDC20 to cell cycle progression. 3. This provides mechanistic support for IAA-stimulated protist proliferation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression under IAA treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because authors explicitly connect it to auxin-regulated growth processes.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of aldA/B among protist-associated bacterial genomes supports bacterial IAA production, which is associated with increased protist growth and cell size.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Genes associated with tryptophan-dependent IAA biosynthesis, including aldA/B, were present in 90% of protist-associated bacterial genomes. 2. Many genomes encoded components of multiple canonical IAA pathways. 3. Bacterial IAA production was validated by colorimetric assay and LC-HRMS at the culture level.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genome annotation; Salkowski indole assay of isolates; LC-HRMS quantification of IAA in protist cultures; bacterial depletion assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Interkingdom signal that promotes protist growth, increases cell size, and delays encystment.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper analyzed pathway presence at genome-community level, not by single-gene knockout.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: metagenomics; bacterial whole-genome sequencing; LC-HRMS metabolite profiling; NCBI BioProject PRJNA1090919

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports bacterial IAM-pathway capacity for IAA biosynthesis in protist-associated communities.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAM-pathway genes were among the canonical IAA biosynthesis genes detected in protist-associated bacterial genomes. 2. IAA biosynthesis genes overall occurred in 90% of genomes. 3. Culture-level IAA accumulation required bacteria and tryptophan.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genome annotation; Salkowski indole assay; LC-HRMS; bacterial depletion assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific gene IDs per genome were not listed in the main text.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports bacterial IAM-pathway IAA production associated with protist growth stimulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAM route was one of three canonical bacterial IAA biosynthesis pathways detected. 2. IAA production was common among isolates and all protist cultures accumulated IAA when supplemented with tryptophan. 3. Depleting bacteria abolished IAA detection in Colpoda culture.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genome annotation; Salkowski indole assay; LC-HRMS; bacterial depletion assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin signal promoting protist growth and delayed encystment.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main text does not resolve contribution of this branch relative to IPA or IAN in any individual isolate.
